statement.p: In a forest, the number of deer can be modeled by the function f(t)={\frac{120t+630}{0.4t+7}}\text{,} where t stands for the number of years from now. Answer the question with technology.
After years, there would be approximately 200 deer living in the forest.

solution.p: Use technology to graph the function f(t)={\frac{120t+630}{0.4t+7}}\text{.}

To locate the point whose y-value is 200\text{,} we graph a second function y=200\text{.} They have an intersection (19.3,200)\text{.} This point implies there will be approximately 200 deer living in the forest after 19.3 years.
